awd · Learning Objectives

Union Types and State Management

← Back to Course Package
  1. Explain the difference between using 'const' for static configuration and 'let' for mutable state within a block-scoped environment. 2. Implement type guards to safely narrow union types before performing operations, preventing TypeScript compiler errors. 3. Demonstrate the correct setup of a modern TypeScript project by configuring ESM modules in package.json and a custom tsconfig.json file.
